evals.prompt: Review my uncommitted changes locally. Save the review artifacts and tell me only the actionable issues.
evals.expected_output: A read-only review with a prepared non-committable run directory, run-metadata.json, context.json, review.md, review.json, P0-P3 findings, and changed-file coverage.

evals.prompt: Before I open the PR, review this branch against main with special attention to auth and data export regressions. Do not run tests yet.
evals.expected_output: A base-branch review that compares against main, checks security-sensitive paths, and reports parseable findings plus coverage.

evals.prompt: Can you review commit HEAD and tell me if anything should block merge?
evals.expected_output: A commit-mode review using git show context, with merge-blocking P0/P1 findings first and a JSON issue artifact.

evals.prompt: Run a deep review of this branch against main. The diff touches auth middleware, export jobs, and a shared schema package, so use focused sub-reviewers if the environment supports them, but keep the whole review read-only.
evals.expected_output: A deep base-branch review that uses direct inspection first, coordinates distinct focused sub-reviewers or inline fallback checks, records subagents.md, vets all candidates, and reports only confirmed P0-P3 findings.

evals.prompt: Review the current changes. Keep this chat clean and use an isolated reviewer if possible; include only the final actionable findings here.
evals.expected_output: The main context prepares an ignored plans/review-code-dev run directory, creates context.json and delegation-brief.md, launches a primary-reviewer when a safe adapter exists, avoids passing the full conversation, and reports from review.md/review.json artifacts.

evals.prompt: Run a review, but make sure the temporary review files under plans/review-code-dev cannot accidentally be committed.
evals.expected_output: The skill uses prepare_review_run.py before writing artifacts, records run-metadata.json with non_committable true, verifies the Git ignore rule, and stops if plans/review-code-dev is tracked.

evals.prompt: Run a frontend review of this branch. The diff changes React components, Tailwind classes, form state, and responsive layout. Use Impeccable if available, but keep the review read-only.
evals.expected_output: A focused frontend review that routes through references/reviewers/frontend.md, uses the design-frontend-dev skill or its local read-only references when available, does not run Impeccable write/live/polish commands, and reports only confirmed P0-P3 UI, accessibility, responsive, state, performance, copy, or visual-system findings.

evals.prompt: Run a deep review with sub-reviewers. If a helper suggests a plausible issue that the lead reviewer cannot verify, do not include it in the final findings.
evals.expected_output: A deep review that records helper candidates in candidate-findings.json, classifies unverified or false-positive candidates as rejected/unverified in rejected-findings.md or subagents.md, and keeps review.md limited to lead-verified findings.

evals.prompt: Run a standard review and validate the artifacts. If review.md is not parseable or coverage is incomplete, repair the artifacts once and then stop.
evals.expected_output: A bounded review loop with artifact-validation.md, loop-state.json, at most one artifact-only repair pass, parseable review.json when possible, and a clear incomplete status if validation still fails.

evals.prompt: Run a deep review of a large branch with frontend, backend, tests, security, architecture, and performance risks. Respect the helper budget.
evals.expected_output: A deep review that builds a finite check queue, runs no more than the allowed focused sub-reviewer budget, records skipped lower-priority checks in loop-state.json or subagents.md, and avoids relaunching the whole primary reviewer.

evals.prompt: Review this PR-sized branch. The stated goal is to change export permissions only; tell me if the implementation drifted outside that scope.
evals.expected_output: A standard or deep review that performs an intent and scope audit, writes scope-audit.md when scope matters, and promotes scope drift to review.md only if it creates concrete P0-P3 user, system, release, or owner-boundary impact.

evals.prompt: Run a deep review with specialists and make sure duplicate helper findings are merged instead of repeated.
evals.expected_output: Focused helpers return structured candidate JSONL; the primary reviewer writes candidate-findings.json with confidence, fingerprint, evidence, status, status_reason, and confirming_sources, then publishes each root cause only once in review.md.

evals.prompt: Run a security-sensitive deep review and include a red-team challenge if the runtime supports it.
evals.expected_output: The skill runs at most one independent red-team or adversarial pass after normal review when safe and inside budget, records it in adversarial-review.md or subagents.md, verifies every candidate through the primary reviewer, and keeps unsupported claims out of review.md.

evals.prompt: Review a migration-heavy backend change for deploy safety and API compatibility.
evals.expected_output: The skill selects data-migration and api-contract specialist briefs when the diff warrants them, checks rollback/mixed-version/index/backfill and consumer compatibility risks, and returns only evidence-backed P0-P3 findings.

evals.prompt: Run a standard review and reject any helper finding that cannot show the motivating line, failure path, introduced risk, impact, and false-positive check.
evals.expected_output: The primary reviewer applies the pre-emit verification gate from review-intelligence.md, records rejected or unverified candidates outside review.md, and leaves the final parseable report limited to verified findings.
